ground_truth.title: PLAYING THEIR PART: What does the market for catering spares look like in 2026?
ground_truth.author: Andrew Seymour
ground_truth.main_content: In a commercial kitchen, every single minute of downtime is a blow to productivity and profits – as any dealer that has received a call from a distressed customer whose equipment has broken down knows only too well. Against this backdrop, where the smallest part can have the biggest impact, it is perhaps little surprise that the spare parts supply chain has become such a critical and innovative element of the catering equipment ecosystem. The ability to deliver replacement parts quickly, accurately and sustainably has never been more vital, which is why specialist spares providers are investing heavily in tools and technology to streamline the process and enhance efficiency. The largest provider of them all, Parts Town UK, has implemented several innovations to improve operations, parts sourcing, inventory, and its overall spare parts service. Our experience, paired with the relationships we hold with our OEM partners, means our order management system algorithm is tailored to ensure the UK's highest stock availability of the most required parts to the industry, explains managing director Mike Barrett. Earlier in the year we introduced a new innovative conveyor system in our distribution centre to optimise the flow of parts by significantly increasing box release efficiencies, enabling faster order fulfilment, which is crucial in meeting our growth rates in OEM parts shipments. Recent years have seen major changes in how quickly and intelligently parts providers can get items to customers that need them. Caren Harvey, managing director at Foodservice Equipment Spares (FES), says more suppliers are using smarter systems to track orders, manage stock and keep customers up to date. It is working on these things itself, building in live stock updates, proper traceability and faster dispatch. Connected equipment and predictive maintenance are also starting to make their mark. With newer machines able to flag when something's wearing out before it fails, people are able to plan better and cut downtime. Jag Thandi, group chief commercial officer at Buttress Group, which owns Caterparts, agrees that the biggest transformation in the sector is happening behind the scenes. Innovation in predictive data, digital cataloguing and AI-driven technology are allowing parts providers to anticipate demand before the customer even picks up the phone. At Caterparts, automation will support everything from replenishment triggers to compatibility verification, meaning customers spend less time searching and more time servicing their clients. We have also made significant investments in state-of-the-art automation through our new vertical lift management systems. This is a major leap forward in both speed and accuracy. The system delivers a 99% pick accuracy rate and can process a complete order in just 12 seconds, dramatically improving fulfilment efficiency and reducing human error. Parts Town's business intelligence platform provides real time visibility into stock movement, seasonal trends and product in-stock performance. Data from connected equipment is starting to influence the industry and it is an evolving area. As operators adopt smarter, and more connected foodservice equipment, we expect that usage-based insights will start to play a larger role in predicting parts demand, particularly for routine maintenance and wear and tear parts. All of these trends points to a market where customers are definitely changing the way they think about spare parts procurement. There is now a greater focus on planning ahead and a deeper appreciation of the value that effective spare parts management can bring to a business. The biggest opportunity is using data and experience to help customers plan better and avoid downtime. Predictive maintenance, better visibility and more joined-up service options are all making a difference, especially among the growing band of customers favouring repair over replacement. The main challenge, meanwhile, is complexity. The variety of equipment models, part variations, and fragmented supply channels can make standardisation difficult. At its core, the future of the spares market will be defined by one thing: predictive precision. It is the ability to deliver the right part, at the right time, through intelligent systems and connected insights, keeping kitchens running, one part at a time. Parts Town UK agrees that the continuous advancement of AI and digital e-commerce will play a transformative role in how parts are sourced, ordered, and managed. It is imperative to make the spare parts identification process as simple as possible for customers. Working with our OEM partners, we have introduced serial number look-up tools to aid the technician with the spare parts selection process. As equipment and kitchens becomes more connected and data improves, it is only natural that it will support more accurate stocking, better service planning, and reduced equipment downtime.

The system delivers a 99% pick accuracy rate and can process a complete order in just 12 seconds.
